feed.title: Pronounce — recent dictionary additions
feed.subtitle: How engineers actually pronounce project / product / jargon names. Community-maintained.

entry.title: Janus — JAY nuhs
entry.summary: Janus is pronounced "JAY nuhs" (/ˈdʒeɪnəs/). DeepSeek's unified multimodal model (Janus / Janus-Pro) that decouples visual encoding for understanding vs generation. Named after the two-faced Roman god of doorways: JAY-nuhs, stress on the first syllable.

entry.title: Mochi — MOH chee
entry.summary: Mochi is pronounced "MOH chee" (/ˈmoʊtʃi/). Genmo's open-source text-to-video model (Mochi 1). Pronounced like the Japanese rice cake: MOH-chee, not "MOTCH-ee" or "moh-CHAI".

entry.title: ColPali — kol PAH lee
entry.summary: ColPali is pronounced "kol PAH lee" (/koʊlˈpɑːli/). Late-interaction visual document retrieval: ColBERT-style multi-vector matching over PaliGemma image patches. Said kol-PAH-lee — "Col" from ColBERT, "Pali" from PaliGemma (PAH-lee, the Buddhist canonical language).

entry.title: Nunchaku — NUN chah koo
entry.summary: Nunchaku is pronounced "NUN chah koo" (/ˈnʌntʃɑːkuː/). MIT Han Lab's 4-bit inference engine that runs SVDQuant-compressed diffusion models. Named after the martial-arts weapon (nunchucks): NUN-chah-koo.

entry.title: SVDQuant — ess vee dee kwant
entry.summary: SVDQuant is pronounced "ess vee dee kwant" (/ˌɛsviːdiːˈkwɑːnt/). 4-bit post-training quantization for diffusion models that shunts weight+activation outliers into a low-rank branch. SVD = Singular Value Decomposition, read letter-by-letter then "quant": ess-vee-dee-KWANT.

entry.title: ModernBERT — MOD ern bert
entry.summary: ModernBERT is pronounced "MOD ern bert" (/ˈmɒdərnbɜːrt/). 2024 modernized BERT-style encoder (8k context, RoPE, FlashAttention, GeGLU). Spoken MOD-ern-BERT — "BERT" as the single word "bert", never spelled out B-E-R-T.

entry.title: Genie — JEE nee
entry.summary: Genie is pronounced "JEE nee" (/ˈdʒiːni/). DeepMind's foundation world model (Genie 1/2/3) that turns a single image or prompt into a playable, interactive environment. Pronounced like the lamp genie: JEE-nee.

entry.title: Medusa — muh DOO suh
entry.summary: Medusa is pronounced "muh DOO suh" (/məˈduːsə/). Speculative-decoding method that adds multiple extra decoding "heads" so a model drafts several future tokens itself (no separate draft model). Named after the Gorgon Medusa with many snake-heads: muh-DOO-suh.

entry.title: Kontext — KON text
entry.summary: Kontext is pronounced "KON text" (/ˈkɒntɛkst/). Black Forest Labs' in-context image generation+editing model (FLUX.1 Kontext). "Kontext" is German for "context"; said KON-text, stress on the first syllable.

entry.title: crate — krayt
entry.summary: crate is pronounced "krayt" (/kreɪt/). Rust's unit of compilation / the package you publish to crates.io. Pronounced exactly like the wooden box "crate": KRAYT, one syllable, rhymes with "great" and "plate". Not "krah-tay" or "crayt-ee". (Asked by a non-native speaker in Discussion #34.)

entry.title: rustc — rust see
entry.summary: rustc is pronounced "rust see" (/ˈrʌstsiː/). The Rust compiler. The "c" is for "compiler" and is almost always read as the letter C: RUST-see (rust + C). A minority say "rust-cee" identically; "rustk" (one syllable) is heard but uncommon. Compare gcc/clang usage where the trailing letter is spoken.

entry.title: clippy — klip ee
entry.summary: clippy is pronounced "klip ee" (/ˈklɪpi/). Rust's official linter (run via `cargo clippy`). KLIP-ee — named after Clippy, the Microsoft Office paperclip assistant; same pronunciation as that mascot.

entry.title: rustup — rust up
entry.summary: rustup is pronounced "rust up" (/ˈrʌstʌp/). The Rust toolchain installer and version manager. Read as the two transparent parts: RUST-up (rust + up), stress on the first syllable. Not "rus-TUP".

entry.title: simplify — sim pluh fye
entry.summary: simplify is pronounced "sim pluh fye" (/ˈsɪmpləfaɪ/). Claude Code /simplify and everyday code review verb; stress the FIRST syllable — "SIM-pluh-fye".

entry.title: compact — kum packt
entry.summary: compact is pronounced "kum packt" (/kəmˈpækt/). The verb (Claude Code /compact — compact the conversation) stresses the SECOND syllable: "kum-PAKT". The adjective/noun ("a compact format") stresses the first: "KAHM-pakt".

entry.title: resume — rih zoom
entry.summary: resume is pronounced "rih zoom" (/rɪˈzuːm/). The verb (claude --resume, resume a session): "rih-ZOOM". Don't confuse with the noun résumé "REZ-uh-may" — different word.

entry.title: init — ih nit
entry.summary: init is pronounced "ih nit" (/ɪˈnɪt/). Clipped "initialize" (git init, npm init, /init). Most say "ih-NIT"; "IN-it" also common; some spell it out I-N-I-T.

entry.title: exec — ig zek
entry.summary: exec is pronounced "ig zek" (/ɪɡˈzɛk/). Clipped "execute" (codex exec, docker exec, exec()): "ig-ZEK".

entry.title: sandbox — sand bahks
entry.summary: sandbox is pronounced "sand bahks" (/ˈsændbɑːks/). Isolated execution environment: "SAND-bahks".

entry.title: headless — hed luss
entry.summary: headless is pronounced "hed luss" (/ˈhɛdləs/). Headless browser / headless CMS: "HED-luss".

entry.title: deprecate — dep ruh kayt
entry.summary: deprecate is pronounced "dep ruh kayt" (/ˈdɛprəkeɪt/). "DEP-ruh-kayt" — three syllables, no "-shee-". Often mixed up with "depreciate" (deh-PREE-shee-ayt), which is a different word.

entry.title: review — rih vyoo
entry.summary: review is pronounced "rih vyoo" (/rɪˈvjuː/). Code review, /review: "rih-VYOO", stress the second syllable.

entry.title: commit — kuh mit
entry.summary: commit is pronounced "kuh mit" (/kəˈmɪt/). git commit: "kuh-MIT", stress the second syllable.

entry.title: rebase — ree bayss
entry.summary: rebase is pronounced "ree bayss" (/ˈriːbeɪs/). git rebase: "REE-bayss".
